behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: HTTP handler + signature verifier + enqueue path. A pure unit test of HMAC comparison would miss accidental forwarding. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required; in-process HTTP invocation with an instrumented queue dependency reproduces the failure. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires 401 and no-forwarding, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the app in-process, send a webhook with mismatched `X-Signature-256`, assert status 401, and assert queue `enqueue` was not called and no persisted queue record exists.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering bug across handler and queue persistence boundary. Minimal real-failure setup wires real handler to queue implementation (or test double with controllable write completion) and checks response timing relative to write completion. No real OS/network/subprocess dependency is stated. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on write-before-ack ordering, not infrastructure-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process request to webhook endpoint with queue write intentionally delayed; verify HTTP 200 is not emitted until write resolves and queue contains the event first.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between request handler, queue handoff, and async processor lifecycle. In-process wiring with a blocked downstream consumer catches incorrect awaiting. No requirement for real network/OS/subprocess. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about await/non-await semantics, reproducible without external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a valid webhook while downstream worker is blocked/sleeping; assert response returns immediately after enqueue and before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time concurrency/SLA behavior (`within 3 seconds wall-clock`) under load from earlier slow events. Event-loop scheduling, actual server I/O, and contention can create failures that in-process fakes or mocked timers hide. Integration-brake check upgrades to System due to real timing/infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process, generate concurrent slow prior events, then send a new valid webhook over real HTTP and measure end-to-end latency; assert response <3s wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Idempotency failure appears at interaction between handler dedupe logic and queue persistence/state store. Single-function unit tests can miss duplicate suppression across requests. Real OS/network/subprocess is unnecessary; local in-process app + persistent test store is sufficient. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real infrastructure effects beyond local module/state wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Send same event ID twice to in-process endpoint; assert first request enqueues once, second request does not create a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates error propagation/translation across handler and queue boundary. Minimal setup is queue module returning a typed `full` condition and asserting HTTP mapping. No real OS/network/subprocess needed. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about status/header mapping, not real transport failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke webhook with queue configured to return `QueueFull`; assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header are present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets the queue component with real persistence semantics (local DB/filesystem) rather than a mock, so multi-module integration is required. System not mandatory unless enforcement depends on external process/OS primitives beyond local persistence. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified by behavior text specifying capacity enforcement logic, reproducible with local persistent store wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with capacity N against a local persistent store, enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue N+1 and assert explicit capacity rejection and no extra persisted record.
